display.purpose: Marketing Home hero only; never inside the product shell.

headline.purpose: Page titles and major section heads.

body.purpose: Prose and descriptions; cap analysis prose at 65-75ch.

label.purpose: Uppercase field labels and column heads. A utility, not a section eyebrow.

mono.purpose: Code, diffs, hashes, tabular figures only. Signals 'this is code or an exact value.'

shadows.name: card-rest
shadows.purpose: Resting card shadow. Soft, neutral, low. Structural only.

shadows.name: card-hover
shadows.purpose: Hover lift on interactive cards and floating layers (menus, dialogs).

shadows.name: glow-DEPRECATED
shadows.purpose: DEPRECATED. Legacy colored glow (--shadow-glow-*, .glow-primary, pulse-glow). Do not use in new work; PRODUCT.md rejects decorative glow.

motion.name: ease-out
motion.purpose: Default exponential ease-out for state transitions. No bounce, no elastic.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single primary action per view. Solid Signal Indigo.

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Neutral action. Surface fill with a hairline border.

components.name: Stat Card
components.description: Dashboard metric tile. Flat at rest, lifts 1px on hover with a neutral structural shadow.
components.html: Analyses this month1,284of 1,000 quota

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Labeled field. Focus shows an indigo ring, never a glow.

components.name: Status Badge
components.description: Pill status token mapped to the similarity scale (info/success/warning/error).
components.html: Low · 32% Review · 64% High · 91%

components.name: Sidebar Nav Item
components.description: Sidebar link. Active state is a full indigo tint fill — NOT a left border stripe.

components.name: Similarity Score Ring
components.description: Signature instrument dial. Combined-similarity 0-100 ring, stroke colored by the similarity scale (green <50 / amber 50-79 / red >=80), number in tabular mono.

narrative.overview: Clone Lens is an instrument, not a marketing surface. Every screen is a readout: it reports what was measured, on what scale, and with how much confidence, then lays the evidence beside the number so a reviewer can act on it and defend the call. Restraint is the aesthetic; the interface recedes and the evidence steps forward. 'Premium' is carried by precision, spacing, and typographic care, never by decoration. The palette is a calibrated slate with two working signals (an indigo and a cyan for the measurement axes), the type is a single well-tuned Inter with JetBrains Mono reserved for code, and depth is nearly flat, spent only where structure genuinely changes. The system explicitly rejects the glow, glassmorphism, and gradient-text costume it inherited; those are legacy, on the way out, not the target.
keyCharacteristics: Calibrated luminous-slate canvas; two working signals (Signal Indigo, Diagnostic Cyan) used for meaning, not decoration.
One type family (Inter) across the whole UI; JetBrains Mono for code only; IBM Plex Sans Arabic for the full Arabic RTL surface.
Near-flat elevation; a single restrained neutral shadow marks true elevation, never a colored glow.
Semantic similarity scale — green (low) to amber (review) to red (high) — applied consistently to scores, badges, and rings.
Bilingual and RTL-correct by construction; code and data stay left-to-right inside an Arabic layout.

rules.name: The One Signal Rule
rules.body: Signal Indigo carries no more than ~10% of any screen's pixels. It marks the one primary action or the current selection and then stops. Its rarity is what makes it read as important.

rules.name: The Meaning-Only Color Rule
rules.body: Every non-neutral color must encode a fact — an action, a state, a similarity band, a chart axis. If a color is present only to look premium, delete it.

rules.name: The Calibrated Scale Rule
rules.body: Green then amber then red always maps to the same similarity bands (roughly <50 / 50-79 / >=80), everywhere: score ring, badges, radar dots, history rows.

rules.name: The Fixed-Scale Rule
rules.body: Product type is fixed rem, never fluid clamp(). Only the Home hero may be fluid.

rules.name: The One-Voice-of-Type Rule
rules.body: No second sans, no display face in the app. Hierarchy is weight (400/600/700) and size.

rules.name: The No-Glow Rule
rules.body: Shadows are neutral and structural. A shadow tinted with a brand hue, or any glow used as decoration, is forbidden. If it looks like the element is emitting light, it is wrong.

rules.name: The Structure-Over-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Reach for a 1px hairline or a tonal surface step before a shadow. A shadow must justify itself by marking a real z-layer.

dos: Do keep Signal Indigo to <=10% of any screen — one primary action or the current selection, then stop.
Do draw structure with a 1px hairline (#dadde7) and tonal surface steps before reaching for any shadow.
Do keep shadows neutral and structural, marking only real z-layers (dropdowns, dialogs, hover lift).
Do set body and secondary text in Ink (#1a1e2e) / Slate Mute (#636b7d), verified >=4.5:1 (AAA where feasible); never lighten body text for elegance.
Do carry one type family (Inter) across the UI; hierarchy via weight and size; JetBrains Mono for code only.
Do map every semantic color to the same similarity band everywhere (green/amber/red), and always pair color with text or icon so it survives color blindness.
Do ship every interactive component with default, hover, focus-visible, active, disabled, and loading states, and a prefers-reduced-motion alternative for every animation.
Do keep code, diffs, and data left-to-right inside the Arabic RTL layout.
donts: Don't use decorative glow or neon accents — retire --shadow-glow-*, .glow-primary/accent, and pulse-glow.
Don't use glassmorphism or blur as a default surface — .glass (frosted backdrop-filter) is not a resting-panel treatment.
Don't use gradient text or rainbow accents — .text-gradient-brand (background-clip: text) is banned; emphasize with weight, size, and one solid color.
Don't build the interchangeable SaaS template — identical icon-card grids and a big-number gradient hero.
Don't use a border-left/border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe (the current .nav-link-active stripe) — use a full background tint instead.
Don't round cards past 20px or buttons past 12px; over-rounding reads as toy, not instrument.
Don't tint a shadow with a brand hue or animate a glow.
Don't introduce a second sans or a display font into product UI, or set prose/labels in mono.
